description: Made with Nero d'Avola and Perricone, this offers alluring aromas of red berry, spice and a floral note. The bright, immediate palate offers succulent wild cherry, crushed raspberry nectarine zest and a hint of baking spice alongside zesty acidity that leads to a crisp, clean finish.
title: Tenuta Rapitalà 2015 Rosato (Terre Siciliane)

description: Linear and mineral driven, this intriguing white opens with aromas of beeswax, acacia, citrus, stone fruit and a yeasty whiff of bread crust. On the expressive palate, a mineral note underscores juicy yellow peach, nectarine and apple. There's a lot going on and a lot to enjoy here.
title: I Stefanini 2015 Monte di Fice  (Soave Classico)

description: Briny orange, grapefruit and grape-skin aromas announce an oily adequate palate. This has a gritty salty bitter-citrus flavor profile and a finish that's oddly nutty.
title: Pagos del Rey 2015 Blume Verdejo (Rueda)

description: Foxy candied plum and raspberry aromas come with a strong whiff of wet dog. Choppy in feel on the palate, with weedy foxy berry flavors, this fails to show much of an upside.
region_1: Vino de la Tierra de Castilla
title: Venta Morales 2015 Tempranillo (Vino de la Tierra de Castilla)

description: This wine's delicate nose offers white spring flower, Mediterranean herb and wet stone aromas. They carry over to the fresh palate, along with fruity green apple and pineapple notes while a savory mineral note backs up the finish.
title: La Sibilla 2015 Falanghina (Campi Flegrei)

description: This wine is light and perfumed, with crisp red currants and a tangy orange-zest texture. It dances from the glass, refreshing and poised to finish.
title: François Lurton 2015 Les Fumées Blanches Rosé (Vin de France)

description: This is a gentle and easy-going Chardonnay. It is soft, lightly buttery and conveys apricot and ripe pear flavors. The aftertaste just touches on acidity.
title: Café du Midi 2015 Chardonnay (Vin de France)

description: Ripe dark-skinned berry, French oak, espresso and coconut aromas emerge in the glass. Youthful and taut, the palate doles out Marasca cherry, crushed raspberry, truffle and vanilla flavors, set against a frame of tightly knit, refined tannins.
title: Talosa 2015  Rosso di Montepulciano

description: Here's a luminous red that opens with red berry, sun-baked soil and Mediterranean herb aromas. It's medium-bodied and approachable, delivering wild cherry, star anise and clove flavors, supported by polished tannins.
title: Molino di Sant'Antimo 2015  Rosso di Montalcino

description: Produced by the winemaking team of Château Giscours in Margaux, this is finely ripe blend of Sémillon and Sauvignon Blanc. With rich tropical fruits and lime flavors, it is balanced and already delicious. Drink now.
title: Petite Sirène 2015  Bordeaux Blanc

description: This spicy rosato opens with aromas of small red berry, blue flower and a whiff of baking spice. The bright, juicy palate offers wild cherry, raspberry, cinnamon and a hint of white pepper alongside fresh acidity.
title: Guerrieri Rizzardi 2015 Classico  (Bardolino Chiaretto)

description: Chignin is one of the cru villages of Savoie. This wine is crisp, mineral and tight, with delicate layers of lemon and grapefruit acidity. Lively and bursting with fruit, it's ready to drink.
title: Domaine les Fils de René Quénard 2015 Les Cantates Chignin Jacquère (Savoie)
winery: Domaine les Fils de René Quénard

description: This attractive blend of Grenache and Cinsault is fruity with lively acidity and a crisp, tangy texture. Raspberry and orange zest hang happily together. Drink now.
title: Domaine Grand St Paul 2015 Rosé (Mediterranée)

description: Rounded and warm, this wine shows ripe yellow fruits and spice from the wood aging. It's richly endowed, full of toast and with a generous texture. The minerality and acidity are a hint in this rounded wine. Drink from 2020.
title: Louis Latour 2015 Les Referts Premier Cru  (Puligny-Montrachet)

description: This very underripe expression of Pinot Noir will win hardcore fans of that style. Tart raspberry meets with green peppercorns, underbrush and Kalamata olives on the nose. The palate is also full of olives, from Kalamata to green, with damp sagebrush, forest floor and more green-peppercorn flavors.
title: Waxwing 2015 Lester Family Vineyards Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)

description: The wine is smoky with plenty of toastiness. At the same time, the bright red fruits are right up-front to give the wine its crispness and acidity. Drink this wine from 2018.
title: Château Petit-Freylon 2015 Cuvée Sarah  (Bordeaux Supérieur)

description: The wine is beautiful—ripe with generous red fruits and with balanced acidity. It has concentration, juicy acidity and a smoky background. This is going to be an opulent wine, rich in tannins and in fruit. Drink from 2022.
title: Albert Morot 2015 Les Toussaints Premier Cru  (Beaune)

description: Powerful tannins and wood flavors dominate the wine at this young stage. It has plenty of the richness of the vintage to promise a generous future. Packed with ripe red-berry fruits and balanced acidity, it will be a very fine wine. Drink from 2024.
title: Château de Santenay 2015  Clos de Vougeot

description: It is not clear while a simple appellation Bourgogne should need a wax capsule and heavy bottle. It all makes it that much harder to get to the warm ripe wine inside. With its strawberry fruit that is laced with acidity, the wine is generous, reveling in the fine 2015 vintage. Drink from 2018.
title: Arnaud Baillot 2015  Bourgogne

description: This is a very fresh medium-bodied wine that overflows with ripe raspberry and blackberry flavors. It's the opposite of a serious age-worthy wine, but wonderful to drink now for its effusive fruit flavors that come along with just enough tannin for support on the palate.
title: AVA Grace Vineyards 2015 Fruity Silky Soft Merlot (California)

description: Ripe, fleshy, minerally blackberry and black-cherry aromas are pure, deep and suggest high quality that's confirmed later on. In the mouth, this Mencía from an excellent vintage is racy and pumped up, with acidic energy and tannic grab. Roasted black-fruit flavors come with notes of spice cake and salt. A long, full finish confirms how good this is; drink through 2022.
title: Losada 2015 Mencía (Bierzo)

description: This wine is ripe, soft and fruity. The acidity, already well integrated, gives a crispness that is balanced by yellow fruits with touches of pineapple. This will be a full wine, very characteristic of the year. Drink from 2019.
designation: Petit Clos Blanc des Theurons Premier Cru
title: Domaine d'Ardhuy 2015 Petit Clos Blanc des Theurons Premier Cru  (Beaune)

description: Winemaker Billy Wathen makes so many single-vineyard wines that he's able to play around with styles, and uses this bottling to explore the lower alcohol side of Pinot Noir. Extremely floral aromas of rose petals, hibiscus and carnation meet with light raspberry on the elegant nose, while the lighter-bodied palate mixes slightly sour red fruit with cedar, thyme and oregano.
title: Foxen 2015 La Encantada Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: The Côtes d'Auxerre, west of Chablis, is the last outpost of Chardonnay in Burgundy. That results in this cool style of wine, light and fruity with apple and green-plum fruitiness. There is a zest to the wine, a lightly mineral texture and attractive final acidity. Drink now.
title: Bailly-Lapierre 2015  Bourgogne Côtes d’Auxerre

description: Fleshy, ripe red and black cherry fruit streams from the glass. This smooth, easy red shows all the ripeness of the 2015 vintage and just enough freshness to balance it. The finish is juicy and full-fruited.
title: Rosenhof 2015 Reserve Zweigelt (Burgenland)

description: Dark, tangy black-plum skin and tart boysenberry meet with purple flowers and rosemary on the nose of this bottling, which promises plenty of life in the cellar. The palate is lightly bodied but firmly structured, with tannic depth upholding the dark purple fruits and subtle black spice. These flavors will concentrate and intensify with time. Drink 2018–2025.
title: Waxwing 2015 Ascona Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)

description: Winemaker Ed Kurtzman manages to bring restraint to this often very ripe region. This bottling shows subtle white-flower buds and the slightest hints of coconut cream on the nose. Delicate flavors of honeysuckle and Key lime are subtle and will intensify with time.
title: August West 2015 Peterson Vineyard Chardonnay (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: A strong effort, this medium-bodied wine combines hints of nettle leaf with bold fruit flavors of nectarine, pineapple and grapefruit. There's creamy texture and ample concentration on the midpalate, followed by a long, silky finish.
title: Marisco Vineyards 2015 The King's Favour Waihopai Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: Rich red cherry, plum and raspberry tones lead the nose of this fruit-forward wine, with hints of cocoa and cola spice dancing in the background. Those notes follow through to the satiny palate, framed by soft tannins that lead into a solid, medium-length finish.
title: La Forge Estate 2015 Pinot Noir (Pays d'Oc)

description: A bright, tight and crisp wine that is is mineral, tangy and refreshing to drink. It hints at the ripe fruits that will develope through 2017.
title: Quinta de Santiago 2015 Alvarinho (Vinho Verde)

description: Fresh, primary red cherry and raspberry flavors are punchy and bright on this easygoing Pinot Noir for everyday enjoyment. It's not a complex wine but pristinely fruity, balanced neatly by a crush of cranberry acidity. The finish lingers elegantly marked by fine, feathery tannins.
title: P.J. Valckenberg 2015 Undone Pinot Noir (Rheinhessen)

description: Bruno Copéret has produced an impressive wine from this small vineyard on his property. It is still young, full of smoky wood and broad red-fruit flavors. The tannins and the concentration are considerable, although the wine also has a more stylish edge. Drink from 2019.
title: Domaine de Roche-Guillon 2015 Clos de la Tour  (Fleurie)

description: This ultra rich style could be mistaken for a Chardonnay. Light gold in color, heady aromas of lemon curd, butter, ripe banana and bruised apple lead into a palate of baked stone fruit and toast, with medium to low acidity.
title: Fern Ridge 2015 Sauvignon Blanc (Marlborough)

description: This delicate rosé offers a subtle bouquet of muddled cherry, carnation and mentholated berry. The light and slightly astringent palate boasts tangy, tart notes of currant and lime rind that linger through the medium-length finish.
title: La Vieille Ferme 2015 Rosé (Vin de France)

description: A selection of grapes from several hamlets (hameau in French), this light wine is soft and ready to drink. Lemon and green-herb flavors have a ripe, juicy character alongside flightly flat acidity.
title: Grandissime 2015 Le Hameau Sauvignon Blanc (Vin de France)

description: Lush scents of vanilla bean, raspberry sauce and cherry syrup show on the nose of this ripely fruited Pinot. The palate is light and direct, with virtually no tannic structure. A fleeting finish follows short flavors of juicy red plum and blueberry.
title: Café du Midi 2015 Pinot Noir (Vin de France)

description: With earthy, rustic aromas that translate onto the palate, this wine seems to miss out on some of the fruitiness normally associated with rosés. A few more months in bottle may help.
title: Château Coussin 2015 La Croix du Prieur Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: Dry, lively and fresh, this wine has an attractively restrained range of flavors that includes lemon, white pepper and herbs. It's thirst-quenching and clean in texture, with a fruit-driven acidity that reverberates on the finish.
title: Bliss 2015 Estate Bottled Sauvignon Blanc (Mendocino)

description: This powerful rich wine is finely structured and concentrated. It has weight from solid tannins that contrast with the high tones of the ripe red-cherry fruits. From a single parcel, this cru wine is dense, both firm and fresh. Drink from late 2017.
title: Domaine Chignard 2015 Beauvernay  (Juliénas)

description: Here's a fine expression of this grape, which has become the signature white wine for the Willamette Valley. This marries New World ripeness to Euro styling. Plump apple and pear fruit carries highlights of bee pollen and honey, with excellent grip and length.
title: Joleté 2015 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)

description: This opens with subtle aromas suggesting white flower, hay and dried aromatic herb. The medium-bodied palate offers juicy yellow peach and a hint of grapefruit while a salty hint of mineral marks the close.
title: Venica & Venica 2015 Ronco del Cerò Sauvignon (Collio)

description: Here's a big round white that opens with aromas of tropical fruit, hay and wet stone while the ripe full-bodied palate offers cantaloupe and green melon. You'll also notice the warmth of evident alcohol.
title: Muzic 2015 Sauvignon (Collio)

description: A blend of 60% Chardonnay, 30% Chenin Blanc and 10% Mauzac, this is a bright and lively white, with attractive aromas of white flower, clover, pear flesh and lightly grilled peach. The medium-weight palate is remarkably balanced, with ample acidity to lift the ripe apple, pear and citrus flavors, finishing with a kiss of sweet spice.
title: Château Rives-Blanques 2015 Le Limoux White (Limoux)

description: The aromas of marionberry, licorice and herb bring a sense of purity, along with light whiffs of oxidation. The dark-fruit flavors are a strange mixture of sweet and tart, not entirely coming together. It doesn't quite stand up on its own but should fit in well at the dinner table.
title: Catman 2015 Malbec (Rattlesnake Hills)

description: Ripe and forward black-cherry and berry notes are juxtaposed by swathes of green herb and pepper in this neatly balanced Cabernet Sauvignon. Fresh in fruit and acidity, it finishes with bold tannins and integrated tones of smoked nut and spice.
title: Fox Run 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (Finger Lakes)

description: A blend from a wider area than just Côtes de Provence, the wine is lightly spicy, full of red fruits and citrus acidity. There is a typical touch of pepper that adds extra interest to what is a refreshing wine.
title: Domaine de la Sanglière 2015 Juliette Rosé (Mediterranée)

description: Vines up to 40-years-old are growing on this 13-acre parcel. The wine is lighter than some in this producer's range. At the same time, it is also more mineral and more textured, giving a tight steely wine that will need some time. So drink this wine from 2019.
title: Domaine Fouassier 2015 Clos Paradis  (Sancerre)

description: This pink blend of 64% Grenache, 31% Syrah and 6% Marsanne offers the expected array of bubblegum, pink roses, peach, watermelon and cool steel aromas on the nose. It's wound nice and tight on the palate, with warm notes of strawberry and bubblegum emerging on the midpalate, leveled by lemon-skin tartness and a chalky tension.
title: Kaleidos 2015 Rosé (Paso Robles)

description: This wine is two-thirds Sémillon and one-third Sauvignon Blanc. Aromas of toasted grapenuts, lemon and spice are followed by broad-feeling fruit flavors that show a lightly creamy feel.
title: Swiftwater Cellars 2015 Proprietary White (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Very rich for any Burgundy, this is plump with ginger and pepper as well as yellow fruits. The acidity has been diminished by the ripe fruit, although the wine still has a crisp aftertaste. Drink the wine now.
title: Cave des Grands Crus Blancs 2015  Pouilly-Loché

description: This is a gentle and attractively fruity wine. Ripe and full, it also has a sense of tangy structure and citrus juice. The wine is developing well and will be ready to drink from summer 2017.
title: Domaine de la Denante 2015  Mâcon-Davayé

description: This is an attractive, soft Pinot Noir rosé. With its ripe red-strawberry flavors, it is rich but has plenty of crisp acidity. The balance and juicy, crisp aftertaste are deliciously refreshing.
title: Jean-Max Roger 2015 Cuvée La Grange Dimière Rosé (Sancerre)

description: This Syrah-Grenache rosé is fresh, inviting and lively, starting with scents of strawberry, raspberry, citrus peel and pink florals. Light and bright on the palate, tart berry and melon rind flavors lead seamlessly into the garrigue-kissed finish.
title: L'Ostal Cazes 2015 Rosé (Pays d'Oc)

description: This is labeled as table wine, but given its frizzante with a blend of 48% Müller-Thurgau, 35% Muscat and 17% Riesling, it bears a close resemblance to Asti Spumante. It's off dry, but not too sweet, and has appealing flavors of lemon meringue, vanilla cream and a touch of spice.
title: Season 2015 Transparency Sparkling (Southern Oregon)

description: Pinot Noir specialist Bryan Babcock explores this aromatic white grape, delivering a lush and creamy expression. Poached pear and orange blossom notes combine with a nutty quality on the nose, while the wine's creamy consistency meets buoyant acidity on the palate. Lush apple, pear and cashews flavors complete the picture.
title: Babcock 2015 Simpatico Sauvignon Blanc (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: This one is a typical, off-dry Vinho Verde with a light prickle and the crispest fruit. The wine is very light, very bright and clean with attractive acidity at the end. Drink now.
title: Enoport 2015 Vinhas Altas White (Vinho Verde)

description: This light wine has crisp raspberry fruits, and lively acidity. It is all fruit with only the lightest texture and very ready to drink now.
title: DFJ Vinhos 2015 Casa do Lago Rosé (Lisboa)

description: This crisp, lemon-scented wine has a light texture and the freshest fruit. It comes with apricot and apple flavors. It is lively and bright and ready to drink.
title: Quinta da Alorna 2015 White (Tejo)

description: The mild but slightly tired aromas of apple, honey and haystack announce a soft palate with apple, nectarine and tropical flavors. A yeasty, somewhat hollow finish indicates this is starting to break down. Drink this 80% Chardonnay and 20% Viognier blend immediately.
title: Viñedos Marchigüe 2015 City Wine Reserva Chardonnay-Viognier (Colchagua Valley)

description: This wine features a fresh nose of grapefruit and lemon peel. It is bright on the palate, offering flavors of grapefruit, pear and green apple. It remains crisp and clean across the entire tongue right into the orange zest finish.
title: Sula 2015 Chenin Blanc (Nashik)

description: Tart lime and gooseberry aromas turn plush and plump on the palate of this lip-smackingly ripe spätlese. Apricot and yellow peach flavors penetrate deeply, catapulted by shards of lemon-lime acidity. It's breathlessly light in body, yet lingers long, leaving airy kisses of blossom and fresh herb in its wake. Irresistible already but should improve through 2025.
title: Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt 2015 Braunberger Juffer-Sonnenuhr Spätlese Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

description: Lightly sweet and rich in both flavor and body, this wine has generous ripe pear and peach notes accented with coconut and vanilla. A layered effect to the flavors and an especially creamy texture make it very tempting.
title: Apothic 2015 White (California)

description: This Zinfandel-based wine, which blends 13% Tempranillo and 6% Petite Sirah, shows strawberry, cherry, baking spice and cola aromas. There is decent tannic tension on the palate, and the flavors—sarsaparilla, cola, stewed plum and clove spice—are easily enjoyable.
title: Chronic Cellars 2015 Dead Nuts Zinfandel (Paso Robles)

description: This bottling from the Beckett brothers—bolstered with 14% Syrah—is yet another crowd-pleaser from their stylish, affordable label. Plump black-fruit aromas meet iodine and dried purple-flower notes on the nose. The palate is rich and soft at first, growing tannic over time, with dark purple-fruit flavors that last long into the finish, edged in bright acidity.
title: Chronic Cellars 2015 Suite Petite Petite Sirah (Paso Robles)

description: Forgoing wood aging, this wine is attractively fruity and crisp. Ripe apple and pear flavors fill out its frame of lemon fruit and bright acidity. Pineapple and white peach notes complete the picture. Drink now.
title: Herdade Grande 2015 Colheita Seleccionada White (Alentejano)

description: Modest pear, pineapple and lemon oil scents mark the nose, while the palate suggests marshmallow, pear and melon. This is full bodied and slightly honeyed in style, with a long, plush finish redolent of ripe citrus. Drink now.
title: Clos de L'Oratoire des Papes 2015 White (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: White spring flower, citrus and a whiff of tomato leaf lead the nose. Bright and linear, the palate offers juicy white grapefruit, pineapple and a hint of mineral alongside tangy acidity.
title: Andriano 2015 Floreado Sauvignon (Alto Adige)

description: While plush blackberry, dried fig and cherry aromas are rich and ripe on the nose, this voluptuous Cab is surprisingly structured and taut on the palate. It's rich and luscious in red fruit yet firmly anchored with cranberry acidity and a fine firm fringe of tannins. It's amply lavished in vanilla, spice and toast, but wears these embellishments well.
title: Hound's Tree 2015 Cabernet Sauvignon (North Fork of Long Island)

description: This wine from vineyard close to the Atlantic coast is crisp, herbal and already refreshing. It has a touch of spice along with crisp lemon and warmer pear fruits. Tangy and juicy, it has a fresh, almost salty aftertaste. Drink from early 2017.
title: Cortes de Cima 2015 Sauvignon Blanc (Alentejano)

description: The wine is packed with fruit: blackberry and damson flavors that are given a great lift by the acidity. It has a spicy side to it, adding complexity and filling out the tannins. Drink this wine from 2018.
title: Château Laubes 2015  Bordeaux

description: The wine is structured while also juicy. Its red-berry fruits are in the process of coming together with the tannins to give a rich wine. It has plenty of acidity at the end to give a crisp aftertaste.
title: Château Panchille 2015 Cuvée Alix  (Bordeaux Supérieur)

description: The 16th-century chateau of Saint Lager is on the slopes of the local landmark hill of Brouilly. The wine comes from vines below. It has spice, cherry and blueberry fruits with attractive acidity. The wine, still with a strong tannic edge, will be better from 2017.
title: Château de Pizay 2015 Château de Saint Lager  (Brouilly)

description: A blend of six different varieties with Merlot and Syrah taking the lead, this wine brings aromas of toast, matchstick and cherry. The flavors are luscious and full, backed by lightly tacky tannins.
title: Lone Birch 2015 Red (Yakima Valley)

description: Herbal notes of blossom rise from the glass, with a smoky, flinty accent. The palate presents a honeyed aspect, promising ripe fruit and lusciousness checked by citric pith and zest. It has a pleasant phenolic grip, with the spice of Demerara sugar and fir honey amid the citrus freshness. It tempts with a wild, exotic promise.
title: Domaine Weinbach 2015 Cuvée Laurence Gewurztraminer (Alsace)

description: Showing just a tinge of bronze color, this wine brings aromatic appeal with notes of strawberry and wet rock. The acidity is lively, keeping the focus on the citrus and strawberry flavors. The finish draws out.
title: W.T. Vintners 2015 Parasol Celilo Vineyard Rosé of Pinot Noir (Columbia Gorge (WA))

description: A dark, saturated color is telling, because this is full and smoky on the nose, with aromas of beef jerky, oak and green herbs. Much like it looks, this Carmenère feels full and saturated. Herbal berry and black plum flavors are peppery and lightly green, while notes of tobacco, coffee and carob comprise the finish.
title: Errazuriz 2015 Max Reserva Carmenère (Aconcagua Costa)

description: Stone, lime salt and lemon zest pique the entry of this medium-bodied white, which is well-integrated in terms of oak. High in citrus flavor and pear, it has a gritty texture that gives it added complexity.
title: Kokomo 2015 Peters Vineyard Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: A soft and ripe wine with juicy Alicante Bouschet dominating the open fruity blend. It is immediately drinkable with just enough acidity to give the wine a crisp (although still rich) aftertaste. Drink now.
title: Adega Cooperativa do Cartaxo 2015 Coudel Mor Classico Red (Tejo)

description: This blend of 60% Riesling and 40% Pinot Gris is less than the sum of those parts. In fact, the two grapes cancel each other out. Slightly volatile, it's got lemon-polish aromas, thin fruit and a sour finish.
title: Ram 2015 Le Bélier Blanc White (Willamette Valley)

description: Citrus and match-stick aromas are basic. This feels plump and a bit low on energy. Generic flavors of melon with peppery green notes finish with low acidity.
title: Sombras del Sol 2015 Reserva Sauvignon Blanc (Colchagua Valley)

description: This soft, warm version of Malbec has light tannins and attractive acidity. Fruity and crisp with bright red berry fruit, it will be ready to drink from 2018.
title: Lionel Osmin & Cie 2015 La Réserve Malbec (Vin de France)

description: Just touched by oak, this is a smooth and ripe wine. Tropical fruits, apricots and mango give it a rich character while the acidity holds it all together. Drink now.
title: Adega Cooperativa do Cartaxo 2015 Bridão Reserva Branco White (Tejo)

description: An aromatic, herbal wine with crisp acidity and delicate raspberry flavors coming from vineyards near Saint-Tropez. The wine is tangy and textured giving a tight mineral aftertaste. Its weight shows it is certainly a food rosé. Drink now.
title: Château de Garcinières 2015 Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: The tannins and high toast flavors have taken over this wine. The fruit has been submerged. It is difficult to see this completely balancing out.
title: Château Malbat 2015 C'est la Vie!  (Bordeaux)

description: Big and flavorful, this wine smells and tastes like ripe blackberries and dried plums, and has the firm, moderately tannic texture to go with that richness. Made from a high concentration of Petite Sirah and Syrah, it's quite intense.
title: Naggiar 2015 Black Beast Red (Sierra Foothills)

description: Quite yellow in the glass, this twist-top bottling shows a slight hint of reduction, with aromas of beeswax, lemon peel and yellow grapefruit pith. There's a good sizzle of acidity to the palate and a steely texture, but it's a tad ripe with lemon candy and spun-sugar flavors.
title: Hug 2015 Rancho Vinedo Chardonnay (Santa Barbara County)

description: A ruby color and gamy, roasted aromas of plum and raspberry are the opposite of light and elegant. The palate follows suit, meaning it's full but also acidic. Chunky, slightly burnt berry and currant flavors hollow out on a lightly spiced finish.
title: Apaltagua 2015 Reserva Pinot Noir (San Antonio)

description: Here's a concentrated Valpolicella that opens with aromas of new leather, truffle, French oak and a balsamic note. The firm palate shows raisin, fig and oak-driven spice alongside close-grained tannins. Drink soon to capture the remaining fruit and freshness.
title: Ca' Rugate 2015 Campo Lavei  (Valpolicella Superiore)

description: Baked blackberry, vanilla, caramel and tar on the nose make for a very inviting entry into this rich Syrah from Bibiana González Rave, whose father-in-law, Gary Pisoni, co-developed this vineyard. A firm structure to the sip gives backbone to the lush flavors of dark berry, purple flowers, smoky campfire, maple and bacon fat.
title: Cattleya 2015 Soberanes Vineyard Syrah (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: Round red cherry, deep plum and ripe cranberry fruit mix with ginger spice and wet slate on the nose of this appellation blend from Ed Kurtzman. There's an iron tinge to the palate, giving its wild red cherry and thyme flavors a mineral kick, framed by leathery tannins and zesty acidity.
title: August West 2015 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: Dry apple and lemon aromas are clean and one step above neutral. In the mouth, this barrel-fermented Verdejo feels full and flush, with citrusy acidity. Integrated oak notes accent a core orange flavor on the finish of this finely made Spanish white.
designation: Finca Las Marcas Fermentado en Barrica
title: Carlos Moro 2015 Finca Las Marcas Fermentado en Barrica Verdejo (Rueda)
